name: The White House
subject: ‘Nothing more or less than a recognition of reality’
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a823f30b291410cd57ca764655510efbf43305cb0d8ab894b8075ce55d9c0c39cb949a1c9a5997d10dd60572008607dcc975343ca10bde8c

‘Nothing more or less than a recognition of reality’

President Donald J. Trump made headlines around the world yesterday by announcing the United States will officially recognize Jerusalem as the capital of Israel. The President also directed the State Department to begin preparations to move the U.S. embassy from Tel Aviv to Jerusalem.

“When I came into office, I promised to look at the world’s challenges with open eyes and very fresh thinking,” the President said. “Old challenges demand new approaches.”

The decision drew praise even from frequent critics of the Administration. National Review’s David French called it one of President Trump’s “
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a823f30b291410cd707909430d2cd950c739395897af51e6e16c8395c7df866bcbefc53435d89de34fd40c0decc54878341b65b0d899c4a7
best, most moral, and important decisions” that “strikes a blow against international anti-Semitism.”

“This is nothing more, or less, than a recognition of reality,” President Trump said.

“It is also the right thing to do.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a823f30b291410cdc82c2aaeecd6efdb508d4a6d956d871e82a564dd49e30de8f56135fd401189ca79ad898032e66b010678fa75bf81de7d
Read the President’s full remarks here.

Remembering the second worst attack on American soil

Today marks the 76th anniversary of the surprise attack on Pearl Harbor that claimed 2,403 American lives on a single day in 1941. The President’s latest video tells the story of that fateful event that plunged America into World War II.

“We remember the lives that were lost, the families torn from loved ones, and the heroes who rose to America’s defense,” the President said. “As the smoke billowed that day from our sinking ships, our entire nation rose up to show our enemies America’s unbending strength, might, and resolve.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a823f30b291410cd1f9d3c4389aaf6d8c6bae53e8b0ef01e4cd5ced0402d2a5b78fdd91e404195829cf2f59894267d3e18310d1173cccdf8
Watch President Trump’s video message on Pearl Harbor here.

‘What it means to have hope’

Continuing to celebrate December’s spirit of giving, First Lady Melania Trump and Second Lady Karen Pence visited hurricane-damaged areas of Texas yesterday to meet first responders, visit families in the process of rebuilding, and assist volunteers with their ongoing efforts to help victims. One family in particular, the Zamoranos, offers all of us a stirring example of courage this holiday season.

“I am so impressed by your positive attitudes and resilient spirit,” the First Lady said. “Your family is an example of what it means to have hope.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a823f30b291410cd796a916d471e7fe7ceeb0b2544864820b63fca1ff57c9e497f12c112f1c9eb09bf46b34d872192709ce16deaa56e299b
See pictures from the First Lady and Second Lady’s visit yesterday.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and Vice President Mike Pence deliver a statement on Jerusalem | December 6, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Trump is meeting with Republican members of the Senate, Congressional leadership, and the Chairwoman of the Republican National Committee.

This afternoon, the President also will sign a proclamation for National Pearl Harbor Remembrance Day.

COMING UP

On Saturday, the President will attend the opening celebration for the Mississippi Civil Rights Museum.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a823f30b291410cd29078bdff73d8736961ba02c1f2a8e854a7a3dfd20f242faa092b97ed2ae1bfa0bf567438680ca70c8a915ddaf9920ca
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a823f30b291410cd757cea22807dc13bde623b7eb2e1b4860eadf943196c8569410f20a91960859671dd18197b7bc28330ad68de46fbc818
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d47d5e3545c3b5a5bc3e7c5516275eb71a7bec868448d18014fb413fea483736b9e7043772544db5dd838b1ef2003357f4
Unsubscribe

name: DNC Call Team
subject: They didn't even read the bill, Derek
body:
Make your voice heard today.
---
**Call Republicans in Congress now and tell them to stop trying to scam middle-class Americans and pass a fair tax plan:

** **[CALL NOW](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/GOP-Tax-Plan-Call-Tool1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+CALL+NOW&utm_campaign=em_20171207_ld_nd&source=em_20171207_ld_nd")**
Derek, there's no getting around just how bad this GOP tax scam is for most Americans. Here are the top four take-aways of where things stand:

**#1 Republican senators didn't even read the bill:** Instead, they passed it under the cloak of darkness this past Saturday morning, after its pieces were being redrafted and edited hours before the vote. New Jersey Sen. Cory Booker even hosted a late-night Facebook Live broadcast at his office in which he attempted to read the 500-page text, but couldn't even get a quarter of the way through it before he was forced to return to the Senate floor.

**#2 The GOP tax plan raises the deficit:** Hardworking Americans woke up Saturday morning to a tax plan which raises our deficit by $1.4 trillion -- to the benefit of big corporations and the 1%, and all at the expense of programs like CHIP that help children access health care, the G.I. Bill that helps veterans and their families get a college education, and other important programs that people count on for subsidies to afford health care.

**#3 The GOP tax plan raises taxes on the middle class:** Even while adding to the deficit, this tax plan raises taxes on most middle-class families. Within a decade, nearly half of Americans would see an increase in their taxes.

**#4 Here's what's next:** Right now, Republicans in the House and Senate are working behind closed doors to reconcile the differences between their two plans. They have the power to demand, create, and pass legitimate tax reform that works for all Americans, not just those at the top. But with Paul Ryan and Mitch McConnell running the show, and Trump impatient to sign something, we're not holding our breath for them to do the right thing.

Democratic senators and House members are doing everything they can to fight for better options.

**And we need to do our part too. Call Congress now and tell them that we demand a tax plan that works for EVERY American, not millionaires, billionaires, and corporations:**

**[CALL NOW](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/GOP-Tax-Plan-Call-Tool1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+CALL+NOW&utm_campaign=em_20171207_ld_nd&source=em_20171207_ld_nd")**
We'll keep you updated as the situation changes, Derek. Thank you for helping fight back.

Lauren

Lauren Dillon
Research Director
Democratic National Committee

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523fb7/4067b0a/4d7575248/7e5bb785/1886134540/VEsC/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20171207_ld_nd&source=em_20171207_ld_nd"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523fb7/4067b0a/4d7575248/7e5bb782/1886134540/VEsD/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20171207_ld_nd&source=em_20171207_ld_nd") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523fb7/4067b0a/4d7575248/7e5bb783/1886134540/VEsA/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20171207_ld_nd&source=em_20171207_ld_nd"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

subject: The sexual predator in the White House (sign this)
body:
Tell Congress: Investigate sexual assault allegations against federal officials, starting with Trump (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Sexual_Misconduct_Commission_dems)

Tell congressional leaders:
“It is time to root out sexual harassment and assault in the federal government. Create an independent, bipartisan commission to investigate the sexual assault allegations against Donald Trump, and any other federal official accused of sexual harassment or assault, and commit to holding perpetrators accountable for their actions.”

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Sexual_Misconduct_Commission_dems)

Dear Activist,

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Sexual_Misconduct_Commission_dems)

Like Hollywood, Washington, D.C. is filled with men in powerful positions who for decades have gotten away with sexual misconduct, harassment, and even assault.

Congress has a broken system of reporting that seems designed to punish survivors and let perpetrators off the hook.1 Members of Congress have a responsibility not only to fix this broken system, but to ensure that colleagues, staffers and any member of the executive branch over whom they have oversight are held accountable for gender discrimination, sexual harassment or sexual assault. That includes Donald Trump, who was caught on tape bragging about sexual assault and who has been accused of sexual misconduct by 16 women.2

When Congress has a real commitment to solving a problem, it can create an independent, bipartisan commission to do the work. We need to demand that Democratic leaders push for systemic change and put massive pressure on Trump’s Republican apologists to follow their lead.

Tell congressional leaders: Create an independent bipartisan commission to investigate sexual harassment and assault by federal officials, including Donald Trump. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Sexual_Misconduct_Commission_dems)

For the last several months, women all over the country have been taking the risk to publicly share their stories of sexual abuse and harassment, and to hold perpetrators, and their enablers, accountable. Their courage has sparked what could lead to a radical shift in how we, as a society, collectively respond to and fight back against systemic misogyny, patriarchy and gender inequality.

Congress is now facing its own reckoning. Congresswomen are publicly talking about the harassment and abuse they have endured in their years working on Capitol Hill. Rep. Jackie Speier has told her own story of being assaulted by the chief of staff of the congressional office where she worked when she was 23 years old.3 Women – including former congressional staffers – have accused sitting members of congress of harassment and assault. Sen. Al Franken and Rep. John Conyers both resigned this week after multiple women accused them of harassment and assault. Most Republicans are racing to distance themselves from Roy Moore, their Alabama senatorial candidate who has been accused of sexually assaulting teenage girls.

Democratic leaders in Congress have the opportunity to take the lead in pushing for real change and real accountability. They should demand that Congress empower a bipartisan commission to look into the systemic problems that perpetuate gender discrimination, sexual harassment and sexual assault in Congress and the federal government more broadly. This would include investigating allegations against federal elected officials, including Trump, in a way that prioritizes believing women, respecting survivors and holding perpetrators accountable. Can you add your voice today?

Tell congressional leaders: Create an independent bipartisan commission to investigate sexual harassment and assault by federal officials, including Donald Trump. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Sexual_Misconduct_Commission_dems)

Asking Republicans to stand up for women and hold sexual predators like Trump accountable is a long shot, and not just because their party’s agenda is anti-woman at its core. In order to preserve their party’s advantage and advance their dangerous agenda, they turned a blind eye to reports of Trump’s sexual predation for months leading up to the 2016 election and have similarly given Trump a pass on his Russia ties, his corruption and the way he is using the White House to enrich his family. But right now, the momentum for holding powerful men accountable for using their power to harass, assault and discriminate against women is on our side.

Moore is a toxic candidate. Massive public pressure has forced Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell to not only say that Moore should step aside if he wins the election, but to also voice public support for women who have called out the alleged pedophile.4 Now is the time to keep the pressure on and force Sen. McConnell, Speaker of the House Paul Ryan and other Republican leaders to not only work in a credible bipartisan manner to make real change in Congress, but to deal with the sexual predator who leads their party.

Tell congressional leaders: Create an independent bipartisan commission to investigate systemic misogyny and sexism in Congress and to investigate and hold accountable Donald Trump and any other elected official accused of sexual harassment or assault. Click the link below to sign the petition:

https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Sexual_Misconduct_Commission_dems (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Sexual_Misconduct_Commission_dems)

Thank you for everything you do,

Heidi Hess, Senior Campaign Manager
CREDO Action from Working Assets (http://www.credoaction.com)

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Sexual_Misconduct_Commission_dems)

References:

Michelle Ye Hee Lee and Elise Viebeck, "How Congress plays by different rules on sexual harassment and misconduct (https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/how-congress-plays-by-different-rules-on-sexual-harassment-and-misconduct/2017/10/26/2b9a8412-b80c-11e7-9e58-e6288544af98_story.html?tid=a_inl&utm_term=.4468924ada23/) ," The Washington Post, Oct. 27, 2017. Margaret Hartmann, "What Happened to the 16 Women Who Accused Trump of Sexual Misconduct (http://nymag.com/daily/intelligencer/2017/11/what-happened-to-trumps-16-sexual-misconduct-accusers.html) ," New York Magazine, Nov. 20, 2017. Sunlen Serfaty, "Congresswoman describes sexual assault in #MeToo video (http://www.cnn.com/2017/10/27/politics/jackie-speier-me-too-sexual-assault-harassment/index.html) ," CNN, Oct. 27, 2017. Eli Watkins, "McConnell on Moore: 'I believe the women,' Moore should go (http://www.cnn.com/2017/11/13/politics/mitch-mcconnell-roy-moore/index.html) ," CNN, Nov. 20, 2017.

Their courage has sparked what could lead to a radical shift in how we, as a society, collectively respond to and fight back against systemic misogyny, patriarchy and gender inequality. Congress is now facing its own reckoning. Congresswomen are publicly talking about the harassment and abuse they have endured in their years working on Capitol Hill. Rep. Jackie Speier has told her own story of being assaulted by the chief of staff of the congressional office where she worked when she was 23 years old.3 Women – including former congressional staffers – have accused sitting members of congress of harassment and assault. Sen. Al Franken and Rep. John Conyers both resigned this week after multiple women accused them of harassment and assault. Most Republicans are racing to distance themselves from Roy Moore, their Alabama senatorial candidate who has been accused of sexually assaulting teenage girls. Democratic leaders in Congress have the opportunity to take the lead in pushing for real change and real accountability. They should demand that Congress empower a bipartisan commission to look into the systemic problems that perpetuate gender discrimination, sexual harassment and sexual assault in Congress and the federal government more broadly. This would include investigating allegations against federal elected officials, including Trump, in a way that prioritizes believing women, respecting survivors and holding perpetrators accountable. Can you add your voice today? Tell congressional leaders: Create an independent bipartisan commission to investigate sexual harassment and assault by federal officials, including Donald Trump. Click here to sign the petition. () Asking Republicans to stand up for women and hold sexual predators like Trump accountable is a long shot, and not just because their party’s agenda is anti-woman at its core. In order to preserve their party’s advantage and advance their dangerous agenda, they turned a blind eye to reports of Trump’s sexual predation for months leading up to the 2016 election and have similarly given Trump a pass on his Russia ties, his corruption and the way he is using the White House to enrich his family. But right now, the momentum for holding powerful men accountable for using their power to harass, assault and discriminate against women is on our side. Moore is a toxic candidate. Massive public pressure has forced Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell to not only say that Moore should step aside if he wins the election, but to also voice public support for women who have called out the alleged pedophile.4 Now is the time to keep the pressure on and force Sen. McConnell, Speaker of the House Paul Ryan and other Republican leaders to not only work in a credible bipartisan manner to make real change in Congress, but to deal with the sexual predator who leads their party. Tell congressional leaders: Create an independent bipartisan commission to investigate systemic misogyny and sexism in Congress and to investigate and hold accountable Donald Trump and any other elected official accused of sexual harassment or assault. Click the link below to sign the petition: () Thank you for everything you do, Heidi Hess, Senior Campaign Manager CREDO Action from Working Assets () Add your name: () References: Michelle Ye Hee Lee and Elise Viebeck, "How Congress plays by different rules on sexual harassment and misconduct () ," The Washington Post, Oct. 27, 2017. Margaret Hartmann, "What Happened to the 16 Women Who Accused Trump of Sexual Misconduct () ," New York Magazine, Nov. 20, 2017. name: Mike Huckabee
subject: FW: Intersect | Your Weekly News Summary
body:
Hi Peter,
I wanted to pass this great email along to you.
I started receiving Intersect after joining My Faith Votes as the National Honorary Chairman, and now every week I look forward to their perspective on the most important news topics of the week from a Christian biblical worldview perspective.
If you find it as valuable as I do, you can sign-up at [http://myfaithvotes.org/intersectsubscribe](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fintersectsubscribe&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=1).
-Mike
Sent from my iPhone
* * *
**From:** Megan West, My Faith Votes
**Sent:** Wednesday, December 6, 2017 9:03 AM
**To:** Mike Huckabee
**Subject:** Intersect | Your Weekly News Summary
| | Think. Pray. Act. at the Intersection of Faith & Politics.
---
[ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fintersect_2017_12_06%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=2)
| **In this week's Intersect, read about:**
* _A Major Supreme Court Case Ruling on Religious Liberty_
* _It’s Time to End Congressional Cover-Up Fund – We Need Your Help_
* _2018 Will Be A Critical Year for America_
* _Trump Made Historic Rollback of National Monuments in Utah_

* * *
# Why Masterpiece Cakeshop Case Could Bring a Major Supreme Court Ruling on Free Expression
The Supreme Court heard oral arguments yesterday in a landmark case that could determine if the First Amendment to the Constitution will continue to protect the freedom of artists who wish to act in a manner consistent with their sincerely held beliefs. The case is called Masterpiece Cakeshop v. Colorado Civil Rights Commission. Alliance Defending Freedom is representing the cake shop and its owner, cake artist Jack Phillips. “Phillips said he couldn’t design a custom wedding cake for two men who requested it. He declined because using his artistic talents to celebrate a same-sex marriage would violate his Christian faith and his artistic freedom,” said CEO of Alliance Defending Freedom Michael Farris. [read more](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.foxnews.com%2Fopinion%2F2017%2F12%2F04%2Fwhy-masterpiece-cakeshop-case-could-bring-major-supreme-court-ruling-on-free-expression.html&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=3) **THINK.
** Regardless of whether you believe Jesus would bake the cake or not, I think we can all agree that Jesus has given us a level of liberty to decide whether we would bake the cake. Jesus had quite a band of followers back in his day. The fishermen would have hated the tax-collector, yet both of these groups were represented in Jesus’ small band of followers. We do not definitively know whether Jesus would have baked the cake but we do know that whoever was wrong, grace could make them right. And his grace is more than icing on the cake [(2 Corinthians 9:8).](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.biblegateway.com%2Fpassage%2F%3Fsearch%3D2%2BCorinthians%2B9%3A8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=4) **PRAY.
** Pray for Jack, his family, and his employees as they wait for the court’s decision. Pray for our 9 Supreme Court Justices that they will uphold the First Amendment of the Constitution and allow Americans to have religious liberty to live out the convictions of their faith. **ACT.
** Watch cake shop owner [Jack Phillips story](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fyoutu.be%2FhfshnP_yqsQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=5). It’s critical that as Christians we are aware of cases of religious discrimination so that we can stand united to defend religious liberty and first amendment rights.
* * *
# Congressional Fund to Cover-Up Sexual Harassment
From sea to shining sea, what started in Hollywood with Harvey Weinstein has now impacted Washington D.C. with officials such as Congressman John Conyers. Since 1997, there have been more than 200 secret payouts, totaling $17 million. While not every payout in the fund is for sexual misconduct, one payout is one too many. This fund does more than rectify the wrongs of our elected officials, it enables despicable behavior that would get the majority of us fired from our job. But we don’t simply have a problem of sexual misconduct in Washington. There is another problem the nation would like to ignore. Surveys indicate 79 percent of American men between the ages of 18 and 30 admit to watching porn regularly. 67 percent of men between 31 and 49 admit to it. And concerning men from 50 to 68, 50 percent confess to regular porn viewing. One in three porn users is a woman. As many as 12 percent of all websites contain porn.” [read more](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fcongressional-fund-to-cover-up-sexual-harassment%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=6) **THINK.
** The only thing more reprehensible than a Congressional cover-up fund for sexual allegations is the sheer existence of such a fund. Budgets reveal values and demonstrate foresight. You put aside money for that which is of importance to you. You reflect on the past as you forecast into the coming year how you anticipate spending that hard-earned money. Our budget not only anticipates reprehensible behavior by our elected officials and their staff, they also plan to keep it private. Some can’t keep their hands to themselves but want to keep these despicable actions to themselves. George Washington believed that morality and religion were the pillars upon which our democracy stood. [Proverbs 14:32](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.biblegateway.com%2Fpassage%2F%3Fsearch%3DProverbs%252014%3A32&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=7) edifies his thought. **PRAY.
** Pray first for those in Congress who have been perpetrators of sexual assault to be exposed. Next, pray for a moral awakening in our nation. Our elected officials are a direct representation of We The People. Pray against the sin of porn that has taken root in our society and for a movement of repentance to sweep across our nation **ACT.
** It’s critical that we put an end to the Congressional cover-up fund in Washington D.C. that is intended to silence victims of sexual harassment at the expense of the American taxpayers. Send a message to your elected officials in Washington, [with our easy and quick tool, to abolish this cover-up fund.](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fp2a.co%2FoLXOyU1&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=8)
* * *
# 2018 Will Be A Critical Year for America – But Are We Ready?
Midterm elections historically have always had a lower voter turnout than Presidential elections. The last time in history that midterm election turnout exceeded a presidential election turnout was back in 1838. Yet there is so much at stake in the 2018 midterm elections Christians cannot afford to overlook. Our entire U.S. House of representatives will be holding elections to secure 435 seats. 33 Senate seats are up for grabs, 36 states will be holding gubernatorial races to choose their governor, there are over 728 school board nations in our largest school districts, and roughly 82% of state legislative seats across the country are holding elections. [read more](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F2018-will-be-a-critical-year-for-america-but-are-we-ready%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=9) **THINK.
** In America, we have been given so much freedom –we have taken it for granted by often skipping out on midterm elections. This cannot continue to be our story. You’ve heard us say – there were 25 million evangelical Christians who were registered to vote but failed to cast a ballot in 2012. And there are over 90 million evangelicals in America today. When we unite and take a stand for biblical values, we become the most powerful voting block in the history of our nation...if we vote. In 2018 My Faith Votes will mobilize people of faith across the country, in churches, on University campuses, through television ads, social media, and utilizing voting technology that in 2016 yielded an 89% voter turnout, far surpassing the national average, and far surpassing other voter engagement initiatives. Once again in 2018, it will all come down to Christians taking responsibility to cast their ballots. **PRAY.
** Pray for our brothers and sisters in Christ who have been apathetic to the political process. We are asking you to be part of praying for greater Christian engagement in 2018. When we seek God and bring his light and truth into our communities – we will be amazed at what God can do. **ACT.
**[Pledge to vote in 2018.](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fpledge%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=10) Let’s stand united, millions of Christians across the country, in this important commitment so that we can see America stand boldly for God.
* * *
# Trump Made Historic Rollback of National Monuments in Utah
President Trump departed for Utah on Monday to announce what could be the largest rollback of federally protected lands in history. Trump's decision on the Bears Ears and Grand Staircase-Escalante national monuments would mark an unprecedented use of presidential power to shrink the national monument designations made by two of his predecessors. The decision would shrink the protected lands in Utah by about 2 million acres. President Trump said in his speech, "With the action, I’m taking today, we will not only give back your voice over the use of this land, we will also restore your access and your enjoyment. Public lands will once again be for public use because we know that people who are free to use their land and enjoy their land are the people most determined to conserve their land." [read more](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.usatoday.com%2Fstory%2Fnews%2Fpolitics%2F2017%2F12%2F04%2Ftrump-travels-utah-historic-rollback-national-monuments%2F919209001%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=11) **THINK.
** God has created us in his likeness and admonished us to tend to and care for His creation [(Genesis 2:15).](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.biblegateway.com%2Fpassage%2F%3Fsearch%3DGenesis%2B2%253A15%26version%3DNIV&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=12) This creation is under futility due to the fallen nature of our world, groaning for redemption [(Romans 8:19-21).](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.biblegateway.com%2Fpassage%2F%3Fsearch%3DRomans%2B8%253A19-21%26version%3DNIV&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=13) As God’s agents of reconciliation, we should tend to and care for all of His creation, not limiting ourselves to the crown of creation – humanity ([Leviticus 23:22](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.biblegateway.com%2Fpassage%2F%3Fsearch%3DLeviticus%2B23%253A22%26version%3DNIV&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=14), [Psalm 8:3-6](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.biblegateway.com%2Fpassage%2F%3Fsearch%3DPsalm%2B8%253A3-6%26version%3DNIV&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=15)). However, just as there is a need for discerning priority relative to climate change, there is a particular prioritized order for creation care as well. Robert Manahan developed an ethical framework that follows: whereas nature must obey humanity and humanity must obey God, in turn, God will be beneficent to humanity and humanity will be beneficent to nature. **PRAY.
** Pray for more decisions that give power back to the states. And pray the beauty of God’s creation in Utah continues to bring people to Him. **ACT.
** Watch’s [President Tump’s speech in Utah](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3DOIo3CQ7zxqM&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=16) to learn about the President’s decision and hear the historic announcement. “You marvel at the beauty of God’s great creation,” the President said to the people of Utah. He continued, “The families and communities of Utah know and love this land the best and you know best how to take care of your land.”
---

[ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2Fmyfaithvotes&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=18) [ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.twitter.com%2Fmyfaithvotes&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=19) [ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.instagram.com%2Fmyfaithvotes&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_int_s1&n=20)

body:
|

| | |
Friend,

I wanted to reach out and say thanks for joining my team. Your willingness to sign petitions, share our message with your friends, and even donate to my campaign means so much to me.

We have seen a flurry of activity since President Trump was sworn in, and while at times the negative news can seem overwhelming, we are seeing real results in fighting back against President Trump and the GOP's extreme agenda.

I come from hard working, blue collar parents who showed me that even when things are not going your way, you get up every day and work to make a difference for your family and community. When I first ran for the U.S. Senate in New Mexico, I carried that example with me and today I strive to represent my state with integrity and bring all voices to the table to affect positive change for New Mexico families.

Americans from all walks of life are coming together to stand up for the progress we've made over the last eight years and to boldly show that we will not back down from our values.

To protect our progress over the next four years, it is going to take all of us working together to stand up to the extreme agenda being laid out before us.

I am grateful that I have you on my team, and I look forward to working together to uphold the values we share.

If you have not already, take a second to [_**like my Facebook page**_](http://nm.martinheinrich.com/page/m/4b75bb2c/43d8135f/6c930910/510320bd/103018387/VEsH/ "https://www.facebook.com/VoteMartinHeinrich/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=heinrich&utm_content=1+-+like+my+Facebook+page&utm_campaign=em021316_NEWNAMES&source=em021316_NEWNAMES") and [_**follow me on Twitter**_](http://nm.martinheinrich.com/page/m/4b75bb2c/43d8135f/6c930910/510320bc/103018387/VEsE/ "https://twitter.com/Heinrich4NM?utm_medium=email&utm_source=heinrich&utm_content=2+-+follow+me+on+Twitter&utm_campaign=em021316_NEWNAMES&source=em021316_NEWNAMES")**** for the latest updates on how you can be involved in our movement.

Thanks,

Martin

Peter,

I was looking back over how much we've done this year, and I just had to share these numbers with you:

At this point in 2015, we had endorsed 18 women running for governor, the House, and the Senate.

This year, we've more than doubled that number -- with **a whopping 41 endorsements for those same offices.**

How big that number gets in 2018 depends on you.

**[Donate $3 to help recruit and support pro-choice Democratic women running for office.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6e91/24c69fd9/1570bc3bf/1890d109/4070181588/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171207_email_2018Optimism?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+recruit+and+support+pro&utm_campaign=WQEFEL10N_digital_email_20171207_2018Optimism_inact&source=WQEFEL10N_digital_email_20171207_2018Optimism_inact")**

These endorsements are just the beginning.

Because not only are we dramatically expanding our work on congressional and gubernatorial races for this cycle, we are also targeting 598 state legislative seats next year.

If 2017 is any indication, we could remake the halls of power in ways that have an impact for decades.

But we can't do any of it if we don't have the resources to recruit, train, and support potentially hundreds of women running for office, many of them first-time candidates.

**Donate $3 to continue growing the number of pro-choice Democratic women we endorse:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Remake-The-Halls-Of-Power](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6e91/24c69fd9/1570bc3bf/1890d10a/4070181588/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171207_email_2018Optimism?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgRemakeTheHallsOf&utm_campaign=WQEFEL10N_digital_email_20171207_2018Optimism_inact&source=WQEFEL10N_digital_email_20171207_2018Optimism_inact")**

Thanks,

Lucinda Guinn
Vice President of Campaigns, EMILY's List

name: Donnelly for Indiana
subject: Isn't this disturbing, Peter?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
Last week, Mitch McConnell rammed his disastrous tax reform plan through the Senate despite bipartisan opposition. |
---

| | | Peter,

Last week, Mitch McConnell rammed his _disastrous_ tax plan through the Senate despite bipartisan opposition. It does little more than shift an even larger tax burden onto the middle class while giving billionaires and corporations massive tax breaks.

But why would Mitch McConnell stop at tax breaks for the uber-wealthy? His bill also eliminates crucial parts of the Affordable Care Act, stripping 13 million of their health care, and gutting billions of dollars out of Medicare.

Joe voted against the McConnell tax plan because he's heard from you before that you won't stand against attacks on the middle class and our health care system. **But this bill will continue to be in the news, and Joe wants to know: What you think is the most disturbing part of the McConnell Tax Plan?**
---

Yesterday President Trump made a dramatic shift in American and Middle East foreign policy by declaring Jerusalem, the birthplace of our Judeo-Christian faith, the capital of Israel and announcing the United States plans to relocate the U.S. Israel embassy to Jerusalem. Because of this bold decision, the tension in the Middle East is rising. The tension is a struggle between secularism and Judeo-Christian principles. It’s also growing here in the states. On Tuesday the U.S. Supreme Court heard the case of a Colorado Baker who is fighting for his First Amendment right of religious freedom. The outcome of Jack’s fight will affect all Americans and write a new chapter in American history. You are part of history in the making! Your efforts paved the way for many of the positive changes that are happening in our nation. And now the continuation of this course will come down to who is voted into office in the 2018 midterm elections. **[That’s why we are asking for you best year-end gift today.](http://www3.myfaithvotes.org/2017120701?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171207_apl_s1&n=2)** Judeo-Christian values will not be part of the fabric of America unless Christ-followers stand united in prayer and turn their faith into action at the ballot box this November. name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: the NRA strikes again
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __
Yesterday, just one month after the tragic mass shootings in Las Vegas and in Sutherland Springs, Texas, House Republicans voted to make it easier for violent criminals, domestic abusers and convicted stalkers to carry concealed weapons across state lines.

Let me be clear: this is the VERY FIRST BILL about gun violence since these recent mass tragedies. And it was not to try to do something to address our national epidemic; it was to pass the NRA-backed "Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act" to make guns more available in communities across America.

This bill will not only make us less safe -- it also runs completely counter to years of Republican rhetoric about the importance of states' rights.

[**Sign here to tell the NRA and Congressional Republicans that we will never stop fighting their immoral attempts to flood our streets with even more guns and make our communities less safe >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b8d/70cdc2ba/46d29f24/390263b/2141452282/VEsH/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/condemn-the-concealed-carry-reciprocity-act?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Sign+here+to+tell+the+NRA+and+Congressio&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_171207&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_171207")

Despite the NRA's self-interested defenses of its policies, this bill has nothing to do with protecting Second Amendment rights.

The state restrictions passed are clearly allowed under the Second Amendment -- former conservative Supreme Court Justice and avid hunter Antonin Scalia even said as much.

[**Fight back against the NRA and their disgusting disregard for American lives and the safety of our communities by staying engaged in the push to regain Congress in November 2018. Send the message that we will not back down >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b8d/70cdc2ba/46d29f24/3902638/2141452282/VEsE/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/condemn-the-concealed-carry-reciprocity-act?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Fight+back+against+the+NRA+and+their+dis&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_171207&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_171207")

Dr. Martin Luther King once said, "Only in the darkness can you see the stars." Thank you for persevering in these difficult times.

Chris Van Hollen
U.S. Senator

subject: Must Read: Hupfer Flexing GOP Finance, Muscle
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](http://mailchi.mp/indiana/must-read-hupfer-flexing-gop-finance-muscle?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
| _**_Must Read:_
Indiana Republican Party & Governor Eric Holcomb Shatter Fundraising Records in 2017**_
---
|
---
|
---
| | [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=fedb89765f&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
|
---
| **[Read Here: Hupfer Flexing GOP Finance, Muscle](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=58b81bc2a3&e=1acd9bb3d6)**
**It may be an "off" year for elections, but we've been hard at work at your Indiana Republican Party, breaking fundraising records, recruiting excellent candidates and gearing up for more big victories in 2018 and beyond.

This week Chairman Kyle Hupfer gave an exclusive interview to Howey Politics Indiana, discussing our busy year at the Indiana Republican Party:**

**On Team Holcomb, our one-of-a-kind joint fundraising initiative:**
_"It sends a great message that the governor cares about Republican success up and down the ticket; it shows strength not just for the campaign, but for the party. It’s allowing us to spend more time talking to constituents and supporters. It’s showing up in the fundraising success as well, clearly first-year records for the governor and lieutenant governor for cash on hand for the end of the year, and the party is as strong as it’s ever been for a year immediately after an election in more than a decade."_

**On doing things differently at the Indiana Republican Party:**
_"Hoosiers realize that Indiana is doing things differently as a Republican Party. The governor has a different approach than others have had. The state party is following suit, whether it’s unique state dinners where we invite veterans as our guests and celebrate the military together as we did with our first Team Holcomb Dinner, whether it’s the way the governor approaches his day-to-day time spent, talking with Hoosiers across the state to make sure they feel connected to their state government, it’s showing up in the fundraising and the polling." _

**On defeating Democrat Joe Donnelly in 2018: **
_"Donnelly is not in strong position. The work we’ve done over the past year about what he says in Indiana and what he does in Washington is talking hold. It’s a message that’s getting burned in. We’re going to have a real strong candidate in that race, whoever comes out in the primary. He’ll be able to pull out Trump supporters because Joe Donnelly has voted against tax reform. That’s not going to play real well here in Indiana."_

**On the leadership of Republican women in Indiana: **
_"The Democratic Party in Indiana has tried to create this false narrative of which party is the party of women. We support them. Indiana has long led in supporting women. The Lugar Series is one of the longest-dated female leadership programs in the country. The RNC was looking at it as a model recently to replicate. Our women in Indiana lead in the Republican Party and I expect them to continue."_

This week Chairman Kyle Hupfer gave an exclusive interview to Howey Politics Indiana, discussing our busy year at the Indiana Republican Party: On Team Holcomb, our one-of-a-kind joint fundraising initiative: "It sends a great message that the governor cares about Republican success up and down the ticket; it shows strength not just for the campaign, but for the party. It’s allowing us to spend more time talking to constituents and supporters. It’s showing up in the fundraising success as well, clearly first-year records for the governor and lieutenant governor for cash on hand for the end of the year, and the party is as strong as it’s ever been for a year immediately after an election in more than a decade." Brendan
